methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Copenhagen Atomics is a Series A nuclear energy company that has raised $25M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 11 comparable companies—3 public (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power—though the narrow peer set at identical stage and valuation tier proved insufficient for reliable median benchmarking.

Given data constraints, the stage-progression method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. Using the $25M total raised as a base proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $50M. This single-method result limits confidence and introduces model dependency risk.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $42M to $57M, centered on a $50M median. The range reflects reasonable bounds around the stage-progression output but should be treated as indicative rather than definitive. Material uncertainties include the absence of sufficiently comparable Series A peers in the nuclear energy sector, reliance on a single valuation method, and the early-stage nature of the company's capital deployment. A more robust conclusion would require either additional Series A nuclear energy comparables or supplementary methods (venture-backed IRR hurdles, discounted cash flow analysis on disclosed unit economics). Investors should treat this range as a starting point pending deeper operational diligence.
